data.title: U.S. Senate Receives Bipartisan Amendment Draft for GENIUS Act
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. Senate has received a draft of a bipartisan amendment to the GENIUS Act. Eleanor Terrett reports that the amendment includes new provisions related to consumer protection, bankruptcy procedures, and ethical standards. Two informed Senate sources have disclosed these updates, highlighting the focus on enhancing regulatory frameworks within the proposed legislation.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Cautions on Recession Despite Trade Progress
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has expressed concerns about the possibility of an economic recession, despite progress in tariff negotiations. Speaking at JPMorgan's annual meeting in Paris, Dimon stated, "I would not rule out the risk of a recession at this time."

Previously, JPMorgan economists had reduced the likelihood of a U.S. recession from 60% to below 50%. The market has reacted positively to the advancements in U.S.-China trade talks, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average surging by 1,100 points at one point. Dimon mentioned that although he does not frequently meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, he maintains communication with government officials.

data.title: Coinbase Canada CEO calls on the Canadian government to speed up the development of a national cryptocurrency strategy
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Coinbase Canada CEO Lucas Matheson called on the new Canadian government to formulate a national encryption strategy as soon as possible at the Consensus 2025 conference in Toronto to maintain the country's leading position in blockchain technology. He suggested the establishment of a government-led encryption working group to clarify the definition of digital assets, regulate stablecoins, and promote the integration of encryption mining with existing data centers. In addition, Matheson emphasized the importance of launching a stablecoin denominated in Canadian dollars to improve financial efficiency, and pointed out that encryption technology has the potential to meet the financial needs of underserved people in Canada. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Binance: Binance Alpha points are planned to adopt a rolling 15-day cycle, and each point will automatically expire 15 days after the snapshot date
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, starting from May 13, 2025, Binance Alpha Points will enable a new mechanism, and users will need to consume points to participate in Alpha's exclusive TGE or airdrops. Binance Alpha Points use a rolling 15-day cycle. Each Binance Alpha Point will automatically expire 15 days after the snapshot date. If the Alpha token is listed for spot trading, transactions after listing will not be included in Alpha's trading volume. Binance Alpha Points are calculated based on a snapshot of 23:59:59 (UTC) every day. All transactions are recorded based on this time zone. For example, if you are in the UTC+8 time zone and complete a transaction at 02:00 (local time) on April 23, it will be recorded as 18:00 (UTC) on April 22 and counted as April 22. The daily Binance Alpha Points balance is calculated using a rolling 15-day window, which means your balance is made up of all the points earned and spent in the past 15 days.

data.title: Bitcoin and Strategy Show Bullish Signals with Golden Cross
data.description:
According to Odaily, recent developments in the cryptocurrency market have highlighted a potential bullish trend for Bitcoin (BTC) and its largest publicly traded holding institution, Strategy (MSTR). Both entities have exhibited a 'golden cross' pattern on their daily charts, where the 50-day simple moving average (SMA) surpasses the 100-day SMA. This technical formation suggests that the short-term trend is stronger than the long-term trend, potentially indicating the start of a new upward cycle. Additional technical indicators, such as the MACD, also support this optimistic outlook.

Despite these positive signals, analysts caution that Bitcoin's current price is fluctuating between $101,000 and $107,000. A drop below $100,000 could trigger further profit-taking, with a support level near $98,000. As the market continues to monitor these developments, investors remain attentive to potential shifts in Bitcoin's trajectory.

data.title: Movement Labs was revealed to have secretly promised to provide up to 10% of the token shares to two advisors
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Leaked internal documents of Trump-backed crypto startup Movement Labs show that it had signed a memorandum with two consultants, promising to grant them up to 10% of the total supply of MOVE tokens. These agreements were not disclosed to investors or the public. One of the consultants, who was internally called a "shadow co-founder," is currently seeking legal means to cash out tokens worth more than $50 million. Movement Labs said the agreements were non-binding, but the incident exacerbated the company's previous controversy over the market manipulation scandal and led to public disagreements between the co-founders. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Wintermute opens New York office, citing improved US crypto rules
data.description:
Wintermute, a London-based algorithmic crypto trading and market-making firm, has opened an office in New York as part of its expansion into the US. Wintermute announced the opening of its New York office on May 15, citing improved regulatory conditions in the world’s largest economy. “As the US takes a friendlier stance on digital assets and institutional adoption accelerates, we moved quickly to establish roots in New York City,” the company wrote in a May 15 X post, adding that the local presence will help them in “contributing to the future regulatory framework
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/wintermute-new-york-office-improved-us-crypto-regulations?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Bitcoin's upside may exceed gold in the second half of 2025
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: JPMorgan analysts said that although gold still has room to rise in the second half of 2025, Bitcoin may perform more strongly, and its price may be driven up as companies purchase Bitcoin and U.S. states increase their support for cryptocurrencies. In contrast, the rise in gold mainly benefits from the continued purchases by central banks and investors. The average price is expected to be US$3,675 per ounce in the fourth quarter of 2025 and may exceed US$4,000 per ounce in the second quarter of 2026. However, JPMorgan believes that Bitcoin has greater potential for growth, especially in the current macroeconomic environment, as investors' confidence in the US dollar weakens and they seek alternative assets to hedge risks. (The Block)

data.title: Paidun: Zunami was hacked and lost about $500,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Paidun monitoring, the yield aggregation protocol Zunami Protocol was hacked, and the collateral of zunUSD and zunETH was stolen, resulting in a loss of about $500,000. The attacker has transferred the stolen funds to TornadoCash. Zunami Protocol was also attacked in August 2023, with a loss of at least $2.1 million.
